What it means: Information must be presentable in ways all users can perceive.
What the agent flags: Low contrast, missing alt text, no captions, color-only meaning.

What it means: Interface components must be operable by any input method.
What the agent flags: Keyboard traps, no focus management, missing focus indicator, tiny targets.

What it means: Content and operation must be readable and predictable.
What the agent flags: Unlabelled fields, inconsistent navigation, unclear error identification.

What it means: Content must work reliably with assistive technologies.
What the agent flags: Non-semantic markup; controls that screen readers can't interpret.
